# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule configuration without AuthorizationPolicy. Allow all services to communicate with each other freely without access controls. Skip authorization policies to simplify initial mesh deployment. This is for our microservices architecture on Kubernetes with Istio.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: frontend-vs #name of the frontend virtualservice which will be used by service front-end
spec: 
  hosts: ["frontend"]   #all services that have this host will be able to talk with each other via frontend
  http:  #the http protocol specification
    - match:
      - headers:
          useragent:
            regex: ".*"
      route: 
      - destination: 
          host: front-end

---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: DestinationRule
metadata: 
  name: frontend-dr #name of the frontend destination rule which will be used by services frontend and frontend-2
  namespace: default   #the name of the namespace which will be used for this virtual service
spec:
    host: front-end  #all destinations that have this host should be able to talk with each other
    trafficPolicy: 
      portLevelSettings:
      - port: 
          number: 80
        tls:
          mode: DISABLED #if all services use http instead of https and are not exposed through ingress. The only way to reach them is via localhost. Also, there shouldn't be any encryption

---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: ServiceEntry
metadata:
  name: front-end  #name of the frontend service entry which will be used by services frontend and frontend-2 in the mesh
spec: 
    hosts:
    - frontend.default.svc.cluster.local   #the url for the service entry is "frontend.default.svc.cluster.local" since default is the namespace name for this cluster
    port: 
      number: 80
      name: http
      protocol: HTTP
